title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Mrs. Eastbrook and Colonel Archibald Eastbrooke discuss the disappearance of the Colonel's revolver. Mrs. Eastbrook recalls seeing the gun in a drawer about a week prior, specifically on Saturday the 30th, the day after a robbery at Miss Blacklock's house. The Colonel expresses relief that the gun was seen after the burglary, as he feared it had been stolen by a "Swiss guy" with mob connections. He admits he does not have a gun license and kept the weapon as a war memento. They speculate that Mrs. Bart might have taken it for protection following the robbery, but they decide not to mention the matter.

Later, Miss Marple walks through the village using the vicar's walking stick. She passes Mr. Elliott's antique shop, observing its window display, before entering the "Bluebird" coffee house. There, she meets Miss Dora Bunner. They discuss local residents, including Philly Hems, a widow and tenant, and Edmund Swithinham. Miss Marple inquires about the deep friendship between Miss Bunner and Miss Letitia Blacklock. 

The conversation turns to the hardships of poverty. Miss Bunner becomes animated and emotional while describing the struggles of hunger, inadequate clothing, and the difficulty of finding work and paying rent. She reveals that she wrote to Miss Blacklock after seeing her name in the newspaper regarding a luncheon for Milchester Hospital. Miss Bunner notes that Miss Blacklock was a clever girl who once served as a secretary to a wealthy man named Mr. Goddard, and she reached out to her as an old school friend.
